issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44 px recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44 × 44 px. Its vertical padding is only 6 px, so the effective touch area remains under the recommended size, affecting users who rely on touch input.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes a transition on transform and applies a translateY(-1px) on hover, creating spatial movement. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation, which can cause discomfort for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for any element that receives focus. Keyboard‑only users lose the default focus ring, violating WCAG 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn component suppresses the focus outline (outline: none) in its :focus-visible state without adding a visible alternative such as a box‑shadow or border, leaving button focus invisible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: .btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform on :active)

issuesFound.fix:
Line 430: .btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } 
  Line 440: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` and only an `outline-offset`, providing no visible focus cue. Keyboard‑only users lose the focus indicator, making it impossible to determine which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` selector removes the outline without adding any alternative visual indicator (e.g., box‑shadow or non‑zero outline). As a result, button focus is invisible to users navigating with a keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` applies `transform: translateY(-1px)`, creating spatial movement that does not respect the `prefers-reduced-motion` user preference, potentially causing discomfort for users who reduce motion.

Add a reduced‑motion override:  
Line 430: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the user scrolls down, `stickyHeader` adds the CSS class `headerHidden` to hide the `<header>` element, but it never sets `aria‑hidden="true"` (or the `hidden` attribute). The header remains in the accessibility tree even though it is visually hidden, which can confuse screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In `stickyHeader._onScroll`, after adding the hidden class also set `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing the header remove the attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger may lack `aria‑controls`
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button receives `aria‑controls` **only if** the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer (`_drawer`) does not have an `id`, the button ends up without `aria‑controls`, breaking the relationship between the button and the navigation panel for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer always has an `id` (e.g., `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = 'mobile-nav-drawer';`) and then always set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button missing initial `aria‑pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The theme‑switcher buttons (`[data-theme-toggle]`) only receive `aria‑pressed` when the user clicks them (`themeSwitcher.toggle`). On page load the button has no explicit pressed state, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which theme is currently active.
issuesFound.fix:
After reading the stored theme (`_current = _load();`) call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current));` for each toggle button during `themeSwitcher.init()`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle missing initial `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) set `aria‑pressed` only when clicked (`_onViewToggle`). The initially active button (e.g., “grid”) does not have `aria‑pressed="true"` on load, leaving the current mode ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `productFilter.init()` after determining the initial `viewMode`, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode));` for each view‑mode button.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger missing explicit `role="button"` when not a `<button>`
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()` the code only adds `role="button"` if the trigger is not a `<button>` **and** does not already have a role. However, some triggers are `<a>` elements without a role, which will be announced as a link rather than a button.
issuesFound.fix:
After the existing check, also add `setAttr(trigger, 'role', 'button');` for any non‑button trigger that lacks a role.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is closed, the panel is given the `hidden` attribute but not `aria‑hidden`. While `hidden` removes the element from the accessibility tree, some browsers/assistive technologies rely on `aria‑hidden` for dynamic updates.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.close()` after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` also call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list does not set `aria‑activedescendant` on focus loss
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex()` updates `aria‑activedescendant` on the input when a suggestion is highlighted, but when the list is hidden (`hide()`), the attribute is not removed, leaving a stale reference.
issuesFound.fix:
In `suggestionListRenderer.hide()` add `if (_inputEl) _inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-activedescendant');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav overlay missing `role="dialog"`
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`_overlay`) that appears when the mobile navigation is open is only shown/hidden via CSS but does not have a landmark role. Assistive technologies may not announce that a modal dialog has appeared.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` after locating `_overlay`, set `setAttr(_overlay, 'role', 'dialog'); setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-modal', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Live‑region for search status (`_statusEl`) is not removed from the DOM on page navigation, potentially causing duplicate announcements.
issuesFound.explanation: `_statusEl` is created once and appended to the search input’s parent, but never removed when the search component is destroyed (e.g., on SPA navigation). This can leave an orphaned live region that may receive stray announcements.
issuesFound.fix: In `siteSearch.destroy()` after removing event listeners, also call `if (_statusEl) removeElement(_statusEl);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger but never writes to an `aria-live` region. Users of assistive technology receive no audible cue that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add a live‑region announcement, e.g.:  
```js
liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened'); // in _openInstance  
liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu closed'); // in _closeInstance
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay does not toggle `aria-hidden` when shown/hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element `_overlay` is shown/hidden by adding/removing the `hidden` attribute, but `aria‑hidden` is never updated. Some assistive technologies rely on `aria‑hidden` to remove the overlay from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden via CSS class lacks `aria-hidden` state
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden, it remains perceivable to screen readers because no `aria-hidden` attribute is set, potentially causing confusion.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not set `aria-invalid` on the email field on error
issuesFound.explanation: When the email address is invalid, the error is only shown visually; screen‑reader users are not informed because `aria-invalid` is never set.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger may lack `aria-controls` if the drawer has no `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: If `_drawer` does not have an `id`, the trigger button receives no `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between the button and the navigation panel.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger may lack `aria-controls` when the panel has no `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: If the panel lacks an `id`, the trigger button receives no `aria-controls`, breaking the accessibility relationship.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav overlay missing a landmark role (e.g., `role="dialog"`), reducing discoverability for assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies cannot identify the overlay as a dialog/modal, making navigation harder for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
When creating the overlay, add a role:  
```js
setAttr(_overlay, 'role', 'dialog');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels lack an explicit `role="menu"` or `role="region"` when not already present
issuesFound.explanation: Without a proper ARIA role, screen‑reader users may not understand the purpose of the panel (menu vs. region).
issuesFound.fix:
Detect the intended purpose and set the role:  
```js
if (!panel.getAttribute('role')) {
  setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu'); // or 'region' as appropriate
}
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher buttons do not have `aria-pressed` state synchronized on programmatic theme changes (e.g., when the system preference changes)
issuesFound.explanation: When the OS theme changes (e.g., dark mode enabled), the visual state updates but the `aria-pressed` attribute may become out‑of‑sync, confusing assistive‑technology users.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter “view‑mode” toggle buttons do not announce the mode change via a live region when switched programmatically (e.g., via URL hash)
issuesFound.explanation: Users relying on screen readers may not be aware that the product list layout has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure every code path that changes the view mode calls `liveRegion.announce` with a clear description:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`Product list switched to ${mode} view`);
```

Dropdown‑menu trigger may lack `aria-controls` when the panel has no `id` attribute
Mobile‑nav overlay missing a landmark role (e.g., `role="dialog"`), reducing discoverability for assistive technology
Dropdown‑menu panels lack an explicit `role="menu"` or `role="region"` when not already present
Theme‑switcher buttons do not have `aria-pressed` state synchronized on programmatic theme changes (e.g., when the system preference changes)
Product‑filter “view‑mode” toggle buttons do not announce the mode change via a live region when switched programmatically (e.g., via URL hash)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger `<button>` that opens the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria-controls` attribute linking it to the drawer element, so screen‑reader users cannot discover which region the button controls.
issuesFound.fix: Add `aria-controls="nav-menu"` (or the drawer’s actual `id`) to the trigger button, e.g. `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from AT when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the script only removes CSS classes; it never sets `aria-hidden="true"` or the `hidden` attribute, leaving the hidden menu in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); _drawer.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay not hidden from AT when menu is closed
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`_overlay`) is hidden visually but never receives `aria-hidden="true"` or `hidden`, so assistive technologies still expose it.
issuesFound.fix: When closing the menu, add `_overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); _overlay.setAttribute('hidden', '');` (and remove these attributes when opening).

issuesFound.title: Search input field missing a label
issuesFound.explanation: The `<input>` used for site search has no associated `<label>` or `aria-label`, relying only on placeholder text, which is insufficient for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Either associate a `<label>` element or add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', 'Search site');`.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The “back‑to‑top” button is identified only by its visual icon and lacks `aria-label`, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of its function.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', 'Scroll to top of page');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing `role="dialog"` or `role="region"` when used as a modal overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is presented as a modal navigation panel but does not have a landmark role, which hampers navigation for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: When opening the drawer, add `_drawer.setAttribute('role', 'dialog'); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-modal', 'true');` and remove these attributes on close.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button does not have `aria-expanded` initialized to `false` if the drawer is hidden on page load
issuesFound.explanation: If the page loads with the drawer closed, the trigger button may lack an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` state, causing a mismatch between visual and accessibility states.
issuesFound.fix: During `init()`, ensure `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` is always applied regardless of drawer state.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation toggle button missing `aria-controls`
Mobile navigation drawer not hidden from AT when closed
Mobile navigation overlay not hidden from AT when menu is closed
Search input field missing a label
Scroll‑to‑top button missing an accessible name
Mobile navigation drawer missing `role="dialog"` or `role="region"` when used as a modal overlay

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened the code removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but it never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the associated trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot know the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened the panel is animated and the item receives an open class, but the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never updated to "true". Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect that the answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the open() function (or after the animation begins).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the code calls srUtils.markInvalid which only adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid="true". Thus screen‑reader users are not informed that the field is invalid. This occurs for every field validated via inlineValidator (e.g., newsletter email input, contact‑form name, email, subject, message, consent checkbox).
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, after detecting result.valid === false, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (and ensure aria‑invalid is removed when the field becomes valid).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visible content, but the code never writes a message to an aria‑live region. Users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After setting aria‑expanded to true (and similarly after setting it to false in _closeInstance), update a dedicated live region, e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region (duplicate of existing announcement)
issuesFound.explanation: Although the FAQ open function now sets aria‑expanded, it already announces via liveRegion.announce but the close function does not announce when an item is collapsed, leaving users unaware that the answer has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `liveRegion.announce('Answer collapsed');` in the close() function after the panel is hidden.  

(Note: Issue 6 is optional if the existing code already announces on close; if not, the fix applies.)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance() removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but it never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open); add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); to reflect the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function lacks live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, no status is announced to a live region, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening the panel, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); or a similar message.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, violating the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when the item is opened.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function lacks live‑region announcement for panel state
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands a FAQ item without announcing the change to a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the newly revealed content.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened || `${item.question} – expanded`); or a suitable message.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not remove aria‑hidden from drawer
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the drawer via CSS classes but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, so the element may remain in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); when the drawer is closed.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation close function does not remove aria‑hidden from drawer

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.open() the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute is never set to "true". The button is marked as collapsed only on close, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes the CSS class that hides the drawer but never adds aria‑hidden="true" to the drawer element, nor does mobileNav.open() remove aria‑hidden when showing it. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open() add removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden'); and in mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds the open class to the panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger element, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); in _openInstance().

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden when opened
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not remove aria‑hidden or set it to false, so the panel stays hidden from AT even when visible.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance() add removeAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden'); after removing hidden.

issuesFound.title: Keyboard‑opened dropdown menu does not announce its state via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened with Enter or Space, the code updates the UI but never writes to an aria‑live region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback that the menu opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); or similar within the Enter/Space branch of _dropdownMenu._onTriggerKeydown.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds the open class and animates the panel but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute, leaving the state ambiguous for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); in faqAccordion.open().

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden when opened
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not remove aria‑hidden, so the panel remains hidden from AT even though it is visible.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call removeAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden'); in faqAccordion.open().

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons do not update aria‑pressed when selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle the active class on click but never set aria‑pressed, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _productFilter._onTabClick or after toggling, call setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); for each tab button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not receive aria‑hidden when closed (duplicate of Issue 2 but for the close path)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden via CSS but still exposed to AT because aria‑hidden is never set on close.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure mobileNav.close() adds setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and mobileNav.open() removes it.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑hidden on the panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() adds the hidden attribute but leaves aria‑hidden unchanged, so the panel remains in the accessibility tree after being closed.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance() add setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); after setting hidden.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden when closed
Dropdown menu panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden when opened
FAQ accordion panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden when opened
Mobile navigation drawer does not receive aria‑hidden when closed (duplicate of Issue 2 but for the close path)

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded update on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown register function adds `aria-expanded="false"` but the open/close handlers (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`) never update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button when the panel is shown or hidden, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1029: add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in `_openInstance` and Line 1042: add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function hides the drawer by removing CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element, so the off‑canvas navigation remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer is hidden, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. When opening, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid` on error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` marks fields as invalid visually but only calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which adds `aria-describedby` without setting `aria-invalid="true"`. Assistive technologies therefore do not receive the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` also call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. When valid, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce updated price
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing._updatePlanCard` updates the price text but does not write to any `aria‑live` region, so users of screen readers are not informed when the billing period changes.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the price elements, set `priceDisplay.textContent` on an element with `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` (e.g., a hidden live region) to announce the new price.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge` changes the numeric badge but does not provide an audible cue via a live region, leaving users unaware of the number of matching products after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, update a hidden element with `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` to announce “X products found”.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle button lacks live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode button (`_onViewToggle`) switches between grid and list layouts but does not announce the change to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, set the text of a hidden live region (e.g., `#view-mode-status`) to “List view enabled” or “Grid view enabled”.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is clicked, the corresponding filter is cleared, but no live‑region update informs the user that the filter has been removed.
issuesFound.fix: After `productFilter.setFilter` or `removeTag`, update a live region with a message like “Filter “Category” removed”.

issuesFound.title: Search status messages not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateStatus` sets the text of `_statusEl` but does not ensure the element has `aria-live="polite"` (it is created with that attribute, but the text is not always refreshed after async fetches). In some code paths the element is hidden, preventing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `_statusEl` remains visible (or temporarily shown) when updating its text, and always set `aria-live="polite"`.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload field does not set `aria‑hidden` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a file entry is removed or the upload list is cleared, the corresponding list item is removed from the DOM but the container does not receive `aria-hidden` updates, which can cause residual focusable elements.
issuesFound.fix: After clearing the list, set `listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true')` and remove it when files are added.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay lacks `aria-hidden` handling
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`_overlay`) is shown/hidden with CSS classes but never receives `aria-hidden` toggling, so screen readers may still perceive it as visible when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `open`, call `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'false')`; in `close`, call `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

fpTitles: Active filter chip removal not announced
Search status messages not announced via live region
File‑upload field does not set `aria‑hidden` when hidden
Mobile navigation overlay lacks `aria-hidden` handling

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update `aria‑expanded` when the menu opens or closes
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown controller adds `aria-haspopup="true"` and an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` to each trigger, but the `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions never change `aria‑expanded`. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the submenu is visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The parser creates the trigger element and adds `aria-controls`, but it never sets an initial `aria‑expanded` value. Without this attribute the trigger’s state is undefined for AT.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens or closes the script emits custom events but does not write to any `role="status"`/`aria‑live` element, so screen‑reader users receive no audible feedback.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel expansion/collapse is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item changes visible content but no live‑region is updated, leaving AT users unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance in the dropdownMenu module changes the visibility of a dropdown panel (removes hidden and adds an “open” class) but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Hidden panel not cleared of aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the code removes the hidden attribute but does not remove a previously set aria‑hidden="true", leaving the element still hidden from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Add removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden') when opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the product count badge and empty‑state message but does not write to an aria‑live region, so screen‑reader users receive no announcement of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, set statusEl.textContent (e.g., “12 products found”) on an element with role="status" and aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger button toggles the visibility of its associated panel but never updates the `aria-expanded` attribute. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when the dropdown is initialized and toggle it to `"true"`/`"false"` in `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` respectively.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel missing aria‑hidden handling on hide
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ panel is closed the element is given `hidden` but its `aria-hidden` attribute is never set. Elements hidden with `hidden` are removed from the accessibility tree, but for consistency and to support browsers that ignore `hidden`, `aria-hidden="true"` should be added.
issuesFound.fix: In `close(item)` after hiding the panel, add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open(item)` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria-invalid` on error
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid by adding an error message element, but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input. Assistive technologies cannot announce that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult` when `!result.valid`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear `aria-invalid` on success
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes the error message but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`. The invalid state may persist for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator._renderResult` when `result.valid`, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` (or set to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not set `aria-invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input is validated via `inlineValidator`, which (as above) does not set `aria-invalid`. Users cannot be informed of the error via assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the newsletter email field follows the fix in Issue 4 (set `aria-invalid="true"` on error and clear on success).

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not set `aria-invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields are validated with `inlineValidator`, inheriting the same missing `aria-invalid` handling.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the fix from Issue 4 to all contact form inputs.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in FAQ accordion not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item changes visible content (the answer panel) but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling a panel, update a dedicated live region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Answer expanded for ${item.question}')`).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in dropdown menus not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, the visible content changes but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users may miss the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening/closing a dropdown, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not announce state via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation button toggles the drawer and updates `aria-expanded`, but no live‑region message is provided, which can help users understand the navigation state change.
issuesFound.fix: After calling `open()` or `close()`, add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu closed')`.

fpTitles: Inline validation does not clear `aria-invalid` on success
Newsletter form validation does not set `aria-invalid`
Contact form validation does not set `aria-invalid`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function (and its open/close helpers) change the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger <button> trigger, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') respectively.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria‑controls attribute referencing the drawer’s id, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Assign a unique id to the drawer element (e.g., id="mobile-nav-drawer") and add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer') during initialization.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden drawer lacks aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close hides the drawer by removing CSS classes but never sets aria‑hidden="true". The element remains in the accessibility tree even though it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in mobileNav.open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded on keyboard interaction
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._onTriggerKeydown handles Enter/Space/Arrow keys to open the menu but also fails to set aria‑expanded on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Mirror the fix from Issue 4 for the keyboard‑opened path.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the question button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function does not clear aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close removes CSS classes and hides the panel but does not set aria‑expanded="false" on the button, leaving the state stale for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when closing.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid visually but only adds an error message element; it never adds aria‑invalid="true" to the input, so screen readers are not notified of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation‑failure branch, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and on success remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Because inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid, the required/email validation on the newsletter email field never conveys the error to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the inline validator for _emailEl adds aria‑invalid as per Issue 8.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields lack aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form inputs validated via inlineValidator do not receive aria‑invalid when invalid, violating ARIA error‑state requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 8 to each bound field.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class without aria‑hidden removal on show
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open adds the “is‑open” class to the drawer but does not remove a previously set aria‑hidden attribute, leaving the element hidden from AT even when visible.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open, call removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden'); after adding the open class.

fpTitles: Newsletter form email input lacks aria‑invalid on validation error
Contact form fields lack aria‑invalid on validation error
Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class without aria‑hidden removal on show

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the drawer’s visibility but never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (`_trigger`) never receives an aria‑controls attribute that points to the drawer panel’s id. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the drawer element, ensure it has an id (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer)`) and set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id); during init().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the trigger button does not have aria‑expanded set (defaults to undefined). Users of assistive tech receive no indication of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after the element is cached.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the code removes the is‑open class but does not set aria‑hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden, causing focus traps for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Functions _openInstance() and _closeInstance() show/hide dropdown panels but never modify the aria‑expanded state on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance() add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance() add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, while close() sets it to false. This creates an inconsistent ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix: In open() add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); before animating.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() returns validation results but only adds CSS error classes; it never calls setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true') or removeAttribute('aria-invalid') . Assistive technologies cannot detect errors.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and after a successful validation, removeAttribute('aria-invalid') or set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the validator removes error styling but leaves aria‑invalid set to true, causing persistent error state for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: In the success branch of validate(), call removeAttribute(field, 'aria-invalid') or setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge() changes the text of the count element but does not announce the new total to a screen‑reader. Users cannot know how many products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, set _announceRegion.textContent (e.g., liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)) or give the badge an aria-live="polite" attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle (monthly/annual) does not set aria‑expanded on load
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle button (`_toggleEl`) is a custom switch that changes state but never receives an initial aria‑expanded or aria‑checked value, leaving its state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.init() or after the initial period is applied, set setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', _pricingState.period !== 'month' ? 'true' : 'false'); or use aria-pressed accordingly.

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria‑invalid when in error state (ContactForm)
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form inputs omit aria‑invalid when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={field.state.touched && !!field.state.error} to each <TextInput> instance.

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual marker (*) not hidden from AT (ContactForm “Full name”)

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual marker (*) not hidden from AT (ContactForm “Email address”)

issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the asterisk span.

issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the asterisk span.

issuesFound.title: Button loading prop not recognized – missing aria‑busy (NewsletterForm submit)

issuesFound.fix: Line 754: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Button loading prop not recognized – missing aria‑busy (ContactForm submit)
issuesFound.fix: Line 964: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner element inside Button lacks aria‑hidden when loading

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria‑hidden (visual duplicate)
issuesFound.explanation: The second <ul> is a visual clone for looping but is not hidden from AT, leading to duplicate content being read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 574: `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slides not hidden via aria‑hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: All testimonial slides are rendered without aria‑hidden on hidden slides, so screen readers encounter off‑screen content.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the map, add `aria-hidden={!isVisible}` to the slide container:

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset but provides no visible alternative (such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline). issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which renders content fully visible to all users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This exposes screen-reader-only text (like labels or skip links) to visual users, cluttering the interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 24px for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content meant for screen readers only is exposed to sighted users, cluttering the interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 465: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 513 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 513: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active rule uses transform for a press effect, but no prefers-reduced-motion override disables this motion. Users sensitive to motion will experience it on every button click.
issuesFound.fix: Line 639: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform for a 3D effect, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 872 is empty. The animation persists for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 872: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle uses invalid aria-checked on button element
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's `_syncToggleState` function applies `aria-checked` to the `_toggleEl` element. However, the `init` function (line 2637) only assigns `role="switch"` if the element is NOT a button. If the HTML provides a `<button>` for the toggle, it retains its button role, making `aria-checked` invalid (buttons should use `aria-pressed`).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2675: if (_toggleEl.tagName === 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); } else { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)); }

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison header uses invalid aria-selected on table header
issuesFound.explanation: The `planComparisonHighlighter` module applies `aria-selected` to elements matching `[data-plan-head]`. These are typically `<th>` elements (table headers). `aria-selected` is not valid on the `columnheader` role. If these headers act as tabs to select plans, they require `role="tab"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2735: setAttr(th, 'role', 'tab'); setAttr(th, 'aria-selected', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard progress bar role not enforced
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard` module updates `aria-valuenow` on the `_progressBar` element but does not enforce `role="progressbar"`. It relies on the HTML to provide this role. If the HTML is missing the role, the element will not be recognized as a progress bar by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2810: function _updateProgress(stepIndex) { if (!_progressBar) return; setAttr(_progressBar, 'role', 'progressbar');

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-label fallback
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` module sets `aria-label` on the zone element using a data attribute or a hardcoded string. If the data attribute is missing, it falls back to a generic string. However, it does not check if the element already has an accessible name (e.g., visible label text) before overwriting or adding to it, potentially duplicating announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3079: if (!zoneEl.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-label', zoneEl.getAttribute('data-upload-label') || 'Upload files – click or drag files here'); }

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner focus trap escape deactivation disabled
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent` module activates a focus trap on the banner with `escapeDeactivates: false`. This prevents keyboard users from closing the banner using the Escape key, which is a standard expectation for dialogs and banners. Users are forced to click a button to dismiss.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3397: const trap = createFocusTrap(_bannerEl, { escapeDeactivates: true, onDeactivate: _hideBanner });

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide tabindex management conflicts with native focus
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel` module sets `tabindex="-1"` on inactive slides. While this hides them from the tab order, if a slide contains interactive content (links/buttons), that content becomes unreachable via keyboard when the slide is inactive, even if visually hidden. The interactive children should also be managed or the slide should use `aria-hidden` which implicitly removes children from the tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2465: slide.el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', String(!isActive)); // Remove tabindex line as aria-hidden handles tree removal

issuesFound.title: Site search status element appended without checking existence
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch` module creates a status element for announcements and appends it to the input's parent. If the script runs multiple times or the DOM is manipulated externally, this could result in duplicate status regions, causing screen readers to announce messages twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1685: _statusEl = qs('#search-status'); if (!_statusEl) { _statusEl = createElement('div', { 'aria-live': 'polite', 'aria-atomic': 'true', class: 'sr-only', id: 'search-status' }); if (_inputEl.parentElement) _inputEl.parentElement.appendChild(_statusEl); }

issuesFound.title: Product filter view toggle aria-pressed update scope
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter` module updates `aria-pressed` on all `[data-view-mode]` buttons when one is clicked. This is correct, but it queries the entire document (`qsa('[data-view-mode]')`). If multiple filter sections exist on one page, toggling one might incorrectly update the state of buttons in unrelated sections.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2025: qsa('[data-view-mode]', btn.closest('[data-product-grid]') || document.body).forEach(b => {

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle button aria-pressed update scope
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher` module updates `aria-pressed` on all `[data-theme-toggle]` buttons globally. If multiple theme toggles exist (e.g., in header and footer), clicking one updates all. While likely intended for sync, if they control different scopes (not global theme), this would be inaccurate. Assuming global theme, this is acceptable but rigid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3559: qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { // Ensure all toggles reflect the global state

issuesFound.title: Form analytics observes aria-invalid without filtering false positives
issuesFound.explanation: The `formAnalytics` module uses a MutationObserver to track `aria-invalid` changes. It counts any change to `true` as an error. If the JS validation logic toggles `aria-invalid` rapidly during typing (e.g., debounced validation), it may inflate the error count in analytics, misrepresenting user struggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3255: if (attributeName === 'aria-invalid' && target.getAttribute('aria-invalid') === 'true' && !target.dataset.errorLogged) { target.dataset.errorLogged = 'true';

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions update `aria-expanded` and visibility but do not announce the state change (open/close) to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT are not informed when a menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1850: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 1863: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause state not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.play` and `testimonialsCarousel.pause` functions update `aria-pressed` on the toggle button but do not announce the state change (playing/paused) to screen readers. Users are not informed when auto-rotation starts or stops.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3250: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPlaying || 'Carousel playing');`
  Line 3260: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselPaused || 'Carousel paused');`

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner appearance not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._showBanner` function displays the consent dialog and sets `aria-hidden="false"` but does not announce the banner's appearance to screen readers. Users may be unaware a modal dialog has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4170: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieBannerShown || 'Cookie consent banner opened');`

issuesFound.title: Price updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._updatePlanCard` function updates the visible price text (`plan.priceDisplay.textContent`) when the billing period changes, but this specific price change is not announced. The general period change is announced, but the new price value is not.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.priceUpdated || 'Price updated to ' + target);`

issuesFound.title: Savings message updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._updateSavingsMessage` function updates the visible savings text (`_savingsMsg.textContent`) but does not announce this change to screen readers. Users are not informed of the specific savings amount dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2940: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.savingsUpdated || 'Savings message updated');`

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `statCounterAnimator._animate` function updates the text content of stat elements (`el.textContent`) during animation but does not announce the final value or the update to screen readers. Users miss the updated statistic.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4070: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.statUpdated || 'Statistic updated to ' + _format(cfg.target, cfg));`

issuesFound.title: Viewport reveal elements not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `viewportReveal._reveal` function adds a class to make elements visible but does not manage `aria-hidden`. If elements are visually hidden via CSS (e.g., opacity) before reveal, they remain in the accessibility tree, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4020: `el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');` (And ensure `aria-hidden="true"` is set initially in CSS/HTML).

issuesFound.title: Discount code input validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `discountCodeInput._onApply` function displays an error message in a status element when a code is invalid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field (`_inputEl`). Screen readers do not identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: `if (!result.valid) { _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); }`

issuesFound.title: File upload validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField._validateFile` function returns an error for invalid files, and `_addFile` announces it, but `aria-invalid` is not set on the input or zone element. The field is not programmatically marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850: `if (!validation.valid) { binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); }`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard progress bar updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard._updateProgress` function updates `aria-valuenow` on the progress bar element, but the element lacks `aria-live`. Progress bar changes are not announced to screen readers automatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3630: `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');`

issuesFound.title: File upload progress updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField._renderRow` function creates a `<progress>` element and updates its value, but `<progress>` elements are not live by default. Upload progress changes are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3890: `progressEl.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');`

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison highlight changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `planComparisonHighlighter.highlightColumn` function visually highlights a column in the comparison table but does not announce which plan is now highlighted. Screen reader users are unaware of the focus shift.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3070: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.planHighlighted || 'Highlighted ' + planId + ' plan');`

issuesFound.fix: Line 3928: liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences declined');

fpTitles: cookieConsent._onAccept function does not announce consent decision to screen readers
cookieConsent._onDecline function does not announce consent decision to screen readers

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function sets the panel to visible and updates internal state but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. While the `close()` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, the missing `true` state on open prevents screen readers from announcing that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: In `faqAccordion.open()`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function adds `aria-describedby` to link the error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: In `srUtils.markInvalid()`, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-hidden toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `close()` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes but do not toggle `aria-hidden` on the drawer element. If the drawer is hidden via transform/position (off-canvas), it remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: In `mobileNav.open()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`. In `mobileNav.close()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent._onAccept()` and `cookieConsent._onDecline()` functions hide the banner upon user action but do not announce the result (e.g., "Cookies accepted") via a live region. Users are not confirmed that their preference was saved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3475: In `cookieConsent._onAccept()`, add `liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences accepted.');` before `_hideBanner();`. In `cookieConsent._onDecline()`, add `liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences declined.');`.

issuesFound.title: Statistic counter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `statCounterAnimator._animate()` function updates the visible number and `aria-label` upon completion but does not use a live region to announce the change. Dynamic `aria-label` updates are often not announced unless the element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3285: In `statCounterAnimator._animate()`, inside `onComplete`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${el.textContent}`);` after setting `aria-label`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.init()` function restores the billing period from storage and calls `setPeriod()`, but if the toggle element exists before JS runs, `aria-checked` may not reflect the initial state until interaction. The `_syncToggleState` function updates it, but ensure it runs on init.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1965: In `pricing.init()`, ensure `_syncToggleState(initPeriod);` is called after restoring `initPeriod` to set the initial `aria-checked` state.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list aria-expanded not reset on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch._close()` function calls `suggestionListRenderer.hide()`, which sets `aria-expanded="false"`. However, if the search input is blurred while open, ensure `aria-expanded` is always synchronized. The current code relies on the renderer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 895: In `siteSearch._close()`, explicitly add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` before calling `suggestionListRenderer.hide();` to ensure state consistency.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button aria-pressed not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.init()` function starts auto-play if configured but does not explicitly set `aria-pressed="true"` on the play button in the initial render if auto-play is active. It relies on the `play()` function which is called, but verify order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2855: In `testimonialsCarousel.init()`, ensure `play()` is called after `_playBtn` is assigned so `aria-pressed` is set immediately on load.

issuesFound.title: File upload entry removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField._removeEntry()` function removes the file from the list and announces "removed from upload queue". However, if the upload fails, `_uploadError` announces the error but does not announce that the item is still in the list or removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3125: In `fileUploadField._uploadError()`, ensure the announcement clarifies if the item remains: `liveRegion.announceAssertive(`${entry.file.name}: ${message}. Item retained in list.`);`.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard progress bar aria-valuetext not updated on step change
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard._updateProgress()` function updates `aria-valuenow` and `aria-valuetext`. Ensure `aria-valuetext` is dynamic. It is currently set to `Step ${stepIndex + 1} of ${total}`. This is correct, but verify it fires on every `_goToStep`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2655: In `multiStepWizard._goToStep()`, ensure `_updateProgress(_current);` is called before focus management to announce the step change immediately.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle button missing aria-pressed on init
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher.init()` function sets the theme but relies on click events to update `aria-pressed` on buttons. The initial state of the buttons in HTML might not match the resolved theme.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3625: In `themeSwitcher.init()`, after `_apply(_resolve(_current));`, call `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)));`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator error summary focus not managed on mobile
issuesFound.explanation: The `contactForm._renderErrorSummary()` function focuses the summary element. On mobile, this might scroll the viewport unexpectedly. Ensure `preventScroll` is used if supported or scroll position is managed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2555: In `contactForm._renderErrorSummary()`, change `requestAnimationFrame(() => _errorSummaryEl.focus());` to `requestAnimationFrame(() => _errorSummaryEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }));`.

issuesFound.title: Skip link target tabindex not removed on blur in all browsers
issuesFound.explanation: The `skipLinkManager._processLink()` function removes `tabindex` on blur. In some browsers, this might cause focus to be lost if the element is clicked again before blur. Ensure the event listener is robust.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: In `skipLinkManager._processLink()`, ensure the blur listener uses `{ once: true }` (already present) and verify `tabindex` removal doesn't conflict with native focus management.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu hover close delay not cleared on focus
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._onHover()` function sets a timeout to close on mouse leave. If the user moves mouse out but focuses the dropdown via keyboard, the timeout might still close it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1475: In `dropdownMenu._onHover()`, clear `inst.hoverTimer` on `focusin` events for the panel or trigger to prevent accidental closure during keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function updates the text content of the stat element dynamically but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users miss the counting animation context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700: liveRegion.announce(`Counting up to ${cfg.target}`);

fpTitles: Toast notification not removed from accessibility tree during hide animation
Sticky header not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
Stat counter animation not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Form field aria-describedby references non-existent error ID
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the newsletter email has an aria-describedby attribute pointing to 'newsletter-email-error' when in an error state. However, the parent FormGroup component does not receive the errorId prop required to render the ErrorMessage component with that ID. Screen readers will announce a description that does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1905: <TextInput ... aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />
  Line 1895: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: useField hook missing reset method causing runtime crash
issuesFound.explanation: The useField hook return object does not include a reset method, but both NewsletterForm and ContactForm attempt to call reset() on the returned field object after successful submission. This will cause a JavaScript runtime error, preventing the form from completing its success state and announcement.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is applied to the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". This misidentifies the modal boundary to assistive technology. The overlay div is also interactive (onClick) but lacks keyboard support or role.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2200: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 2205: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: Demo Modal rendered without accessible trigger
issuesFound.explanation: LuminaryPage renders a Modal component controlled by isDemoModalOpen, but the SiteHeader component does not accept or render a trigger button for the onRequestDemo prop passed to it. The modal functionality exists in the DOM but is unreachable by any user interaction.

fpTitles: Form field aria-describedby references non-existent error ID
useField hook missing reset method causing runtime crash
Modal aria-modal attribute placed on overlay instead of dialog
Demo Modal rendered without accessible trigger

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". This prevents assistive technologies from correctly identifying the dialog as modal, potentially allowing users to navigate outside the dialog context.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2475: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
Line 2485: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when invalid, but this ID is never rendered because the parent FormGroup does not pass the errorId prop correctly. Screen readers will announce a non-existent error description.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup fails to render error message
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects errorId and errorMessage props to render the ErrorMessage, but receives a generic error prop instead. This causes validation errors to be invisible to all users, violating WCAG 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2075: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: The social media links in the footer contain visible text ("Twitter", "GitHub", "LinkedIn") but also have aria-label attributes that override the visible text. However, the visible text is not contained within the aria-label value, violating the "label in name" requirement for speech control users. Screen reader users hear "Luminary Studio on Twitter (opens in new tab)" but speech control users cannot activate the link by saying "click Twitter" because the accessible name does not contain the visible text "Twitter".

  Line 882: <a href="https://github.com/luminary-studio" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="GitHub — Luminary Studio (opens in new tab)" target="_blank">
  Line 887: <a href="https://linkedin.com/company/luminarystudio" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LinkedIn — Luminary Studio (opens in new tab)" target="_blank">

issuesFound.fix: <button type="submit" aria-label="Search"> or <button type="submit">Search</button>

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The input lacks a programmatically associated label. Placeholder text is not a substitute for a persistent label, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand the field's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: <label for="newsletter-email">Email address</label> (add before the input) or add aria-label="Email address" to the input

issuesFound.title: Contact email input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The input field has no associated label element, aria-label, or aria-labelledby attribute, leaving screen reader users without context for the required field.
issuesFound.fix: <label for="contact-email">Email address</label> (add before the input) or add aria-label="Email address" to the input

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover state applies a transform (translateY and scale) that is not suppressed in the prefers-reduced-motion media query. While the global media query disables transitions, the spatial transform persists, which may cause issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1306: .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove underlines and rely solely on colour for identification. Without additional visual differentiators such as font-weight increase or border-bottom, users with colour vision deficiencies may not distinguish these links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: Add font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); or add border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; with border-bottom-color on hover/focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox selector `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]` sets an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this small control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size — search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `#site-search button[type="submit"]` sets an explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of 28×28 pixels. While this meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44×44px recommended target size without compensating spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 772-773: `width: 44px; height: 44px;` or add `padding: 8px;` to achieve 44px total size

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size — footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` sets a min-height of 24px with no vertical padding, resulting in a touch target of only 24px height. This meets the minimum but falls below the 44px recommended size without compensating margin or padding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1557: `min-height: 44px;` or add `padding-block: 10px;` to achieve 44px total height

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size — small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `.btn-sm` sets a min-height of 36px with only 6px vertical padding (space-1-5), resulting in a total height of 36px. This falls in the 24–43px range without sufficient compensating spacing to meet the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 639: `min-height: 44px;` or increase padding to `padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-3-5);` (12px vertical)

issuesFound.explanation: The selector `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6` applies `letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight)` which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: `letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal);` or `letter-spacing: 0.01em;`

issuesFound.fix: Line 793: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` sets `text-decoration: none` and the hover state (line 1552) only changes colour, failing to provide a non-colour visual differentiator for links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1552: Add `text-decoration: underline;` or `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`
