issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size — search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28 px, which is below the 44 px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.8). The element has zero padding, so the touch area is not compensated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 607: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size — small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36 px, which is below the 44 px recommended target size. The vertical padding is 6 px (var(--space-1-5)), which is less than the 8 px required to compensate for the smaller height.
issuesFound.fix: Line 716: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20 px, which is below the 24 px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the control difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1313: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability — headings
issuesFound.explanation: The h1–h6 elements use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight), which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces the space between characters, which can impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 95: `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-wrap: balance; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability — hero heading
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-content h1 element uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter), which resolves to -0.05em. This tight spacing can reduce readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 688: `.hero-content h1 { font-size: var(--font-size-6xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-extrabold); line-height: var(--line-height-none); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); text-wrap: balance; margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability — section header
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header h2 element uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), which reduces character spacing and can impair readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 759: `.section-header h2 { font-size: var(--font-size-4xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-extrabold); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); margin-bottom: var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability — step content heading

issuesFound.fix: Line 867: `.step-content h3 { font-size: var(--font-size-xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element has color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#005fcc). The contrast ratio is 2.89:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 425: `.skip-link { position: fixed; top: var(--space-3); left: var(--space-3); z-index: var(--z-toast); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-6); background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-active); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); border: 2px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); box-shadow: var(--shadow-xl); transform: translateY(-200%); transition: transform var(--transition-base); white-space: nowrap; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — stat value
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-value element has color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff) on a gradient background containing var(--colour-brand-primary) (#005fcc). Against the blue portion of the gradient, the contrast ratio is 2.89:1, which fails the 3:1 requirement for large text (48px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1019: `.stat-value { font-size: var(--font-size-5xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-black); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); line-height: var(--line-height-none); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter); margin: 0; }` (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script to change background or add text shadow)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — stat label

issuesFound.fix: Line 1023: `.stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }` (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script to ensure background provides sufficient contrast)

issuesFound.fix: Line 1093: `.pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-active); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-active); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The universal :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for all keyboard-focusable elements that do not have specific overrides, making it impossible for keyboard users to see where focus is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator for buttons when navigated via keyboard. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 438: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28×28 px touch target. This meets the 24×24 px minimum but falls below the 44×44 px recommended size, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 588: `width: 44px; height: 44px;` or add `padding: 8px;` to achieve minimum 44px target

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, creating a 20×20 px touch target that fails the WCAG 2.2 minimum requirement of 24×24 px for pointer targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1105: `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color ..., transform ...), which causes motion when buttons are activated. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this transform transition, potentially triggering vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 414: (add after line 414) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition property, creating motion effects without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the transform transition for users who require reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 552: (add after line 552) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-offset: -3px without defining an outline width or alternative visual indicator. This results in no visible focus ring for elements using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color without an outline width. Without a non-zero outline width, no focus ring is rendered.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets an explicit min-height of 36px (below the 44px recommended minimum) with vertical padding of only 6px (below the 8px threshold that would compensate). This creates a touch target that may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit dimensions of 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto instead of position: absolute and 1px dimensions. This makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers, defeating the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, enabling spatial movement on the :active state. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove text-decoration and only provide a colour change on hover (from var(--colour-neutral-700) to var(--colour-brand-primary)). Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with colour vision deficiencies.

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
Touch target below minimum size
Link underline removed with no alternative

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, removing the default browser focus ring without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page will be unable to see which element currently has focus.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline with outline: none and provides only an outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users on buttons. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Small touch target for small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36px (below the 44px recommended size) with only 6px (0.375rem) of vertical padding, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44×44px area and potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target too small
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets explicit width and height of 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size, and has zero padding, resulting in a touch target that may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width and height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24×24px, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to select the checkbox accurately.

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (for the :active state translateY effect), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial animation for users who have requested reduced motion, potentially causing vestibular issues.

issuesFound.title: Skip link transform animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes transform in its transition property for the translateY animation, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button transform animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition property (for the hover translateY effect), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial animation for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for stats label text
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.75) (75% opacity white) against a gradient background (approximately #6b21a8 to #005fcc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.4:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text at 14px.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for newsletter paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section > p selector uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) (80% opacity white) against a gradient background containing #005fcc and #6b21a8, resulting in a contrast ratio below 4.5:1, which fails WCAG AA for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for table "not included" indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"] selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) against a white or very light blue background (rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.04)), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.26:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for text (even at 20px, which requires 3:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for table "not available" indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) against a white or very light blue background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.26:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for input placeholder text
issuesFound.explanation: The input::placeholder selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against a background of var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.85:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 309: 
  input::placeholder, textarea::placeholder { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); opacity: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight) in the default state, relying solely on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in FAQ content
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dd p a selector sets text-decoration: none in the default state with only an underline on hover, providing no non-colour visual indicator in the default state to distinguish the link from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in footer address
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address a selector sets text-decoration: none without a non-colour visual differentiator in the default state, relying solely on colour change on hover to indicate the link, which is insufficient for WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or background) in the default state, relying solely on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in footer bottom
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p a selector sets text-decoration: none in the default state with only an underline on hover, providing no non-colour visual indicator in the default state to distinguish the link from surrounding text.

issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving no visible focus indicator for high contrast mode users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline-offset: -3px but does not set an outline width or style, resulting in no visible focus indicator. The outline-offset applies to a zero-width outline.

issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 612: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, which is below the 44px recommended size and lacks the minimum 8px padding needed to compensate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: var(--space-2); border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. It also lacks the clip property needed to hide content visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 390: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transition: transform var(--transition-base) for its slide-in animation, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button includes transform in its transition (transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast)), but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 895: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card includes transform in its transition (transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base)), which causes spatial movement on hover, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1012: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base); } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 118: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border in the same rule or a paired rule. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for users in high-contrast mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362-364: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for buttons when navigated via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for the navigation toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 449: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for the search submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for account navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 491: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for feature card links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 653: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for FAQ accordion buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 728: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for footer navigation links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 780: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for social media links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 792: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for the scrollable table region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 805: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 582: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 594: min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, which meets the 24px minimum but may be below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no compensating padding, which meets the minimum but may be below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 788: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 382-392: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 525: (add after existing transition) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img class includes transform in its transition property, which causes motion animation on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 580 is empty and does not disable this transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 580-583: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card class includes transform in its transition property, which causes motion animation on hover. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 710: (add after existing transition) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 127: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces the space between letters and can impair readability for users with visual impairments or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 128: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label class sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% white) against a blue gradient background (approximately #5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio below 4.5:1 for normal text and below 3:1 for large text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"]
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table cells for "Not included" set color: var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails the minimum 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 847: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links inherit text-decoration: none from the global a rule and only change color on hover without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as underline, border-bottom, or background highlight. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text by color alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 487: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The social links inherit text-decoration: none and only change color on hover without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as underline or border-bottom. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text by color alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 790: .social-links a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller opens and closes dropdown panels in response to user interaction (click, hover, keyboard), but unlike other interactive modules (mobileNav, faqAccordion, pricing), it does not write to the liveRegion utility to announce the state change to screen reader users. While aria-expanded is updated, users may not realize the panel has appeared or disappeared without an audible announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1772: Add after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 1786: Add after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form error message has conflicting ARIA live region attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The error message element is created with both role="alert" (which implies aria-live="assertive") and aria-live="polite". This creates conflicting instructions for assistive technologies and may cause inconsistent announcement behavior. role="alert" should be used alone for assertive errors, or use role="status" with aria-live="polite" for polite messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3428: Remove `'aria-live': 'polite',` from the createElement call, or change `role: 'alert'` to `role: 'status'` if polite announcements are intended.

issuesFound.title: Assertive live region incorrectly uses role="status"
issuesFound.explanation: The assertive live region (used for critical announcements) is created with role="status", which is intended for polite status updates. Assertive announcements should use role="alert" to ensure they interrupt the user immediately. The current configuration may delay critical announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1082: Change `role: 'status',` to `role: 'alert',` for the assertRegion wrapper.

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison table column highlighting is mouse-dependent
issuesFound.explanation: The column highlighting feature (hovering over a table header highlights the entire column) is only available to mouse users via mouseenter/mouseleave events. Keyboard users cannot trigger this highlighting, making the visual association between plan features unavailable to them. While not blocking, this creates an unequal experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2689: Add keyboard event listener to support Enter/Space keys on header cells to toggle highlight, or document that this is decorative only. Add `delegate(_tableEl, '[data-plan-head]', 'keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); highlightColumn(e.target.closest('[data-plan-head]').getAttribute('data-plan-head')); } });`

issuesFound.title: Images missing alt text are silently made decorative
issuesFound.explanation: When the auditImages utility encounters images without alt attributes, it sets alt="" (making them decorative) and logs a warning, but does not distinguish between truly decorative images and informative images that are missing alt text. This could result in meaningful images being hidden from assistive technologies without developer awareness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1549: Instead of automatically setting alt="", add a visible CSS class or data-attribute to flag the image for manual review: `img.classList.add('missing-alt');` and console.warn.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger may lack accessible name verification
issuesFound.explanation: While the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-expanded are set on dropdown triggers, it does not verify or ensure the trigger button has an accessible name (aria-label or text content). An icon-only button without aria-label would be announced only as "button" or "menu button" without indicating which menu it controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1729: Add check and warning: `if (!trigger.textContent.trim() && !trigger.getAttribute('aria-label')) { warn('dropdownMenu: trigger button lacks accessible name', trigger); }`

issuesFound.title: File upload zone lacks aria-describedby linking to constraints
issuesFound.explanation: The drop zone is made focusable with role="button" and aria-label, but if there are file type or size restrictions (config.allowedMimeTypes, maxFileSizeMB), these constraints are not programmatically associated with the control via aria-describedby, so screen reader users may not know the restrictions until after attempting to upload.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3615: After creating the zone, add: `if (config.allowedMimeTypes.length || config.maxFileSizeMB) { const descId = srUtils.ensureId(zoneEl, 'upload-desc') + '-constraints'; const desc = createElement('div', {id: descId, class: 'sr-only'}); desc.textContent = \`Accepted types: \${config.allowedMimeTypes.join(', ')}, Max size: \${config.maxFileSizeMB}MB\`; zoneEl.parentElement.insertBefore(desc, zoneEl); setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', descId); }`

issuesFound.title: Wizard step error summary lacks aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: When step validation fails, an error summary is created and focused, but if focus management fails or the user is using a screen reader that doesn't follow focus strictly, the error message may not be announced because the container lacks aria-live. While role="alert" is present on the container, adding aria-live="assertive" ensures immediate announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3844: Add `'aria-live': 'assertive',` to the errorSummaryEl createElement options.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode toggle missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons update the layout of the product grid but do not have aria-controls to programmatically associate them with the grid container they affect. While the visual change is apparent, screen reader users benefit from knowing which region will be affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2214: Add `const gridId = srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'product-grid');` before the event handler, then in the handler add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', gridId);` for the active button.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button may lack accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button is selected via SELECTORS.searchClear but the code does not verify or ensure it has an accessible name. If the button contains only an icon (×), screen reader users will hear "button" without knowing its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2053: Add after selecting _clearBtn: `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_clearBtn.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search'); }`

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle uses incorrect ARIA state attribute for button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The code applies aria-checked to the billing period toggle element regardless of its HTML tag. If the element is a native <button> (common for this pattern), aria-pressed should be used instead. aria-checked is only appropriate for elements with role="checkbox", role="radio", or role="switch". The code adds role="switch" only when the element is not a button, leaving native buttons with an incorrect aria-checked attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3523: Replace setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)); with conditional logic: if (_toggleEl.tagName === 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); } else { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)); }

issuesFound.title: Progress bar ARIA attributes set without ensuring role and accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard updates aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext on the progress bar element, but never ensures the element has role="progressbar" or an accessible name (aria-label). Without these, screen readers may not recognize the element as a progress indicator or announce it properly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4156: Add before setting values: if (!_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) { _progressBar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar'); } if (!_progressBar.getAttribute('aria-label')) { _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Form completion progress'); }

issuesFound.title: Search clear button queried without ensuring accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The JavaScript queries the search clear button from the DOM and attaches an event listener, but does not verify or ensure the button has an accessible name (aria-label or text content). If the HTML uses an icon-only button without aria-label, the button will be inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2847: Add after query: if (_clearBtn && !(_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label') || _clearBtn.textContent.trim())) { setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search'); }

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle buttons queried without ensuring accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The code attaches click handlers to view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) but does not ensure these buttons have accessible names. Icon-only buttons without aria-label will be announced as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3723: Before delegate, query buttons and ensure labels: qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(btn => { if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { const mode = btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode'); setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', `Switch to ${mode} view`); } });

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button queried without ensuring accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button is queried from the DOM. While the code updates aria-pressed and the aria-label dynamically in play/pause functions, it does not ensure an initial accessible name exists if the HTML element lacks text or aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4024: Add after query: if (_playBtn && !_playBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.playCarousel || 'Start automatic slide rotation'); }

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button queried without ensuring accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is queried from the DOM. The code assumes it exists but does not verify it has an accessible name for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1947: Add after query: if (_btn && !(_btn.getAttribute('aria-label') || _btn.textContent.trim())) { setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top'); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle queried without ensuring accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu trigger button is queried from the DOM. The code sets aria-expanded and aria-controls but does not ensure the button has an accessible name (e.g., "Open navigation menu").
issuesFound.fix: Line 1824: Add after query: if (_trigger && !(_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') || _trigger.textContent.trim())) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', STRINGS.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu'); }

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers queried without ensuring accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are discovered from the DOM. The code ensures aria-expanded and aria-controls, but does not verify the trigger has an accessible name, which is required for buttons that open menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2076: Add in register function after getting trigger: if (trigger && !(trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') || trigger.textContent.trim())) { setAttr(trigger, 'aria-label', 'Open menu'); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers queried without ensuring accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are queried from the DOM. The code ensures aria-expanded and aria-controls, but does not ensure the button has an accessible name if the HTML structure lacks text or aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3372: Add in parse function: if (trigger && !(trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') || trigger.textContent.trim())) { setAttr(trigger, 'aria-label', 'Toggle answer'); }

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons queried without ensuring accessible name consistency
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets both aria-pressed and aria-selected on period buttons. These attributes are mutually exclusive for different roles. aria-pressed is for toggle buttons, aria-selected is for tabs and gridcells. Setting both creates conflicting information for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3516: Remove setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); and keep only aria-pressed for toggle buttons, or determine role first and set only the appropriate attribute.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in mobileNav toggles the navigation drawer visibility and CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1140: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the open() function body.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the expanded state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function animates the panel and updates CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as invalid, failing WCAG 3.3.1 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 670: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the addDescribedBy() call in the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function updates the UI and emits a custom event but does not announce the closed state via the live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that the navigation menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1170: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...)` call in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals content dynamically but does not announce the state change via a live region. Users navigating with screen readers are not informed that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');` immediately after setting aria-expanded in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes it from view but lacks a corresponding live region announcement. Screen reader users may not realize the menu has closed and focus has returned to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');` immediately before the `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...)` call in the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle between active and inactive states but the function only updates CSS classes, omitting aria-pressed. This prevents screen readers from announcing the pressed state of the toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2870: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` inside the forEach loop in the _syncTabAriaStates function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer and announces the change via liveRegion, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1767: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1928: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function for FAQ items animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the answer panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3429: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles the active CSS class on filter buttons to reflect the current category, but it does not set aria-pressed to indicate the pressed state. These buttons act as toggle buttons (one active at a time) and require aria-pressed to communicate their state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2898: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Consequently, screen readers do not announce the invalid state of the field to users. This affects all forms using inlineValidator (contactForm, newsletterForm, multiStepWizard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1247: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated to "true". This prevents screen reader users from knowing the menu state. The _closeInstance function sets it to "false", but _openInstance does not set it to "true".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2485: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state of the input to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1892: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function, before the existing addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap and return focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog but does not trap focus within the drawer when open, nor does it return focus to the hamburger trigger button when closed. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2348: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after showing the drawer. Ensure the existing `_trap.deactivate()` call in close() at line 2375 remains to handle focus return.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, no announcement is made to screen reader users. Users are left uncertain whether the menu successfully closed, especially since focus management is also missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the final closing brace of the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, screen reader users receive no confirmation of the state change beyond the aria-expanded update. A polite live region announcement improves usability for complex navigation structures.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` in _openInstance. Line 2508: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is collapsed, there is no announcement to screen reader users. While the open() function announces the expansion, the close() function should similarly announce the collapse to confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4168: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }` before the closing brace of the close function.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent action completion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users accept or decline cookies, the banner dismisses without announcing the action outcome. Screen reader users may not realize their preference has been recorded or whether analytics were enabled or disabled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5825: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieAccepted || 'Cookie preferences saved. Analytics enabled.');` in _onAccept before _hideBanner(). Line 5842: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieDeclined || 'Cookie preferences saved. Analytics disabled.');` in _onDecline before _hideBanner().

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed update
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate the active state via CSS classes, but the aria-pressed attribute is not updated programmatically when the active filter changes. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3546: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop in _syncTabAriaStates, immediately after the existing toggleClass call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. The register() function initializes aria-expanded to "false", but it is never updated when the state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2180: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the end of the _openInstance() function after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`.
  Line 2200: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the end of the _closeInstance() function before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function selects the navigation drawer and trigger button but does not establish an aria-controls relationship between the trigger and the drawer. Screen readers cannot identify which element the trigger controls.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav not initializing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When mobileNav.init() runs, it does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the closed state of the menu on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2085: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input not initializing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function configures the search input as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" when the suggestion list is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2630: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing filter results
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product count and emits a custom event, but it does not announce the filter results to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filter.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2900: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(
    totalMatches === 0 
      ? STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products match the selected filter' 
      : (STRINGS.filterApplied || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`)
  );
  ```

issuesFound.title: pricing not announcing billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates prices and emits an event when the billing period changes, but it does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have switched between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3120: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(
    period === 'year' 
      ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing' 
      : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'
  );
  ```

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu not announcing open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that a dropdown panel has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2180: In _openInstance(), after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');`.
  Line 2200: In _closeInstance(), after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion not announcing open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions expand and collapse FAQ answers but do not announce the state change to screen readers, despite STRINGS.faqOpened and STRINGS.faqClosed being defined in the configuration.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3240: In open(), after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));`.
  Line 3260: In close(), after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close not announcing navigation closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function announces when the menu opens, but when closing the menu, no announcement is made to confirm that the navigation has closed.

fpTitles: faqAccordion not announcing open/close state
mobileNav close not announcing navigation closed

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide the dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger elements (inst.trigger). This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2984: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true;
  Line 2997: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); immediately after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open = true, but unlike the close() function, it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4123: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function toggles visual active states on filter tab buttons but does not set aria-selected="true" (for the tab pattern) to indicate the active tab to screen readers. The buttons only receive CSS class changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3572: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); before toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the prices update visually with animation, but the change in billing period is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3869: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`); after requestAnimationFrame(() => { _pricingState.animating = false; });

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds error message references via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Without this attribute, screen readers do not identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2651: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); immediately after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes error message references from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute from the field, leaving it incorrectly marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2660: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); immediately after the describedby references are cleared (after the if/else block removing the attribute);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount() function configures the search input as a combobox but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion list is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3261: Add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded (JS-A)
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the navigation drawer visibility and body classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the mobile menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3550: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment in the open() function.
  Line 3570: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after the `_open = false;` assignment in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls (JS-E)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute establishing the programmatic relationship with the drawer element (_drawer), which is required for the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3520: After obtaining both _trigger and _drawer, add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded (JS-A)
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element to reflect the open or closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3720: In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`.
  Line 3735: In _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid (JS-D)
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4250: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` at the end of the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid (JS-D)
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4260: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` at the end of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement (JS-G)
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters and the product grid updates, the applyFilters function updates the count badge and empty state visually but does not announce the result count or filter change to screen readers via the liveRegion utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4950: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement (JS-G)
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the setPeriod function updates the displayed prices visually but does not announce the billing period change or new prices to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5520: In the setPeriod function, after updating all plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement (JS-G)
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed via the open() and close() functions, the state change is not announced to screen readers, so users may not realize the accordion action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6050: In the open() function, after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title) : \`\${item.title} expanded\`);`.
  Line 6070: In the close() function, after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title) : \`\${item.title} collapsed\`);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions omit aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not toggle aria-expanded when their panels open or close. Assistive technology users cannot perceive whether the dropdown content is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1800: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside `_openInstance`.
  Line 1822: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;` inside `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid, the function updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` inside the `markInvalid` function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid omits aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are cleared or marked valid, the function removes error descriptions but does not clear aria-invalid (or set it to "false"). Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1360: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the end of the `markValid` function, after the aria-describedby cleanup.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters omits live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count changes but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed how many results match their selection or if the filter produced no results.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2400: Inside the `filterAnimation.run` callback (after `_animating = false;`), insert:
  `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products match your filters' : (STRINGS.filterApplied || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`));`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod omits live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may be unaware that the pricing display has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2620: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing' : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing');` immediately after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);` inside the `setPeriod` function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions omit live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion panels open and close without announcing state changes to screen readers, despite the `announceToSR` parameter existing in the function signature. Users cannot tell when content expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2900: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' ' + (STRINGS.faqOpened || 'expanded'));` at the end of the `open` function before the closing brace.
  Line 2920: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' ' + (STRINGS.faqClosed || 'collapsed'));` at the end of the `close` function before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions omit live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes without announcing its state to screen readers, despite the `announceToSR` parameter existing. Users cannot tell when the navigation becomes available or is dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1680: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` at the end of the `open` function.
  Line 1722: Insert `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the end of the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions omit live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing state changes to screen readers. Users cannot perceive when dropdown content becomes available or is hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1800: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` at the end of `_openInstance` before the closing brace.
  Line 1822: Insert `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` at the end of `_closeInstance` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function omits focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background content, losing their place and potentially accessing interactive elements that should be unreachable while the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1680: Insert the following code immediately before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...)`:
  `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation relies on srUtils.markInvalid which omits aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation errors for the newsletter email field are not conveyed to screen readers because the underlying validation utility does not set aria-invalid. This affects all forms using inlineValidator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: Ensure the fix for Issue 5 (srUtils.markInvalid) is applied. Additionally, verify that `_emailEl` receives `setAttr(_emailEl, ARIA.INVALID, 'true')` when validation fails in `_onSubmit`.

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation relies on srUtils.markInvalid which omits aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Contact form validation errors are not announced as invalid to assistive technologies because the validation utility does not set aria-invalid on failing fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: Ensure the fix for Issue 5 (srUtils.markInvalid) is applied so that `inlineValidator.validate(field)` automatically sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard validation relies on srUtils.markInvalid which omits aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard step validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields, preventing screen reader users from identifying which fields need correction before proceeding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: Ensure the fix for Issue 5 (srUtils.markInvalid) is applied so that `inlineValidator.validate(field)` sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields within the wizard.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField upload progress not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: File upload progress updates the visual progress bar but does not announce percentage changes to screen readers. Users cannot track upload status audibly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3600: Inside the `progress` event listener (where `entry.progress` is updated), add:
  `if (entry.progress % 25 === 0) liveRegion.announce(`${entry.file.name} ${entry.progress}% uploaded`);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch loading state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When search suggestions are loading, the busy state is set visually but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that a search is in progress.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchLoading || 'Searching...');` immediately after `_searchState.loading = true;` and before the try block.

multiStepWizard validation relies on srUtils.markInvalid which omits aria-invalid
