siteSearch loading state not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3840 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`
  Line 3860 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set the initial aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Since the drawer is closed by default, the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" to indicate the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3890 (in init function, after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4050 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 4070 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, dropdown triggers are registered and configured with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but the initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false" for closed dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4120 (in init function, inside register callback): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after ensuring aria-controls is set.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions animate the panels and update state classes but do not synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. The attribute is only set during initialization, so it becomes stale after the first toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6480 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`
  Line 6510 (in close function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input has role="combobox" and manages a suggestion listbox, but the _open and _close functions do not toggle aria-expanded to indicate whether the listbox is displayed. This is required for combobox accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5520 (in _open function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 5540 (in _close function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-haspopup="listbox", but the initial aria-expanded="false" state is not set when the module initializes and the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5580 (in init function, after mounting listbox): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly, annual, etc.) function as toggle buttons but do not have their aria-pressed attribute updated to reflect which period is currently selected. The function only toggles CSS classes.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons where one is pressed at a time, but the function only updates CSS classes and does not set aria-pressed to indicate the active state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6800 (in _syncTabAriaStates function): Add inside the forEach loop:
  `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter result updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product cards and the result count badge are updated, but there is no announcement via an aria-live region to inform screen reader users how many results are now showing or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6900 (in applyFilters, inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the price values update visually across all plan cards, but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region, leaving them unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6150 (in setPeriod function, after updating plan cards): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not returned to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users will lose their place in the tab order and be forced to navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3860 (in close function, before emit): Add `_trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog but does not activate a focus trap when opened. The _trap variable is declared but never assigned or activated, allowing focus to move outside the drawer to background content while it is open.

issuesFound.title: Contact form personal data inputs missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form inputs for first name, last name, email, and company collect personal data but do not have autocomplete attributes (e.g., "given-name", "family-name", "email", "organization"). This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from auto-filling known values.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 7500 (after selecting each field): 
  For contactFirstName: `setAttr(contactFirstName, 'autocomplete', 'given-name');`
  For contactLastName: `setAttr(contactLastName, 'autocomplete', 'family-name');`
  For contactEmail: `setAttr(contactEmail, 'autocomplete', 'email');`
  For contactCompany: `setAttr(contactCompany, 'autocomplete', 'organization');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing proper autofill support for users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7400 (after selecting _emailEl): Add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: productFilter _onTabClick missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle handlers missing live region announcement

issuesFound.fix: Line TestimonialsCarousel slide div: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-modal="true"` attribute is placed on the backdrop overlay instead of the dialog element itself. This may cause assistive technologies to incorrectly handle the modal context or fail to restrict navigation to the modal content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Modal overlay: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line Modal dialog: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to indicate error state but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users when validation fails.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input has `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"` when invalid, but the FormGroup component is not passed `errorId="contact-name-error"`, so the error message element is never rendered with that ID. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: <FormGroup
    label="Full name"
    htmlFor="contact-name"
    required
    errorId="contact-name-error"
    errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.fix:
Line: <FormGroup

    htmlFor="contact-email"
    required
    errorId="contact-email-error"
    errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field broken aria-describedby reference

Line: <FormGroup

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea has `aria-describedby` referencing "contact-message-error" when invalid, but the FormGroup component is not passed `errorId="contact-message-error"`, so the error message element is never rendered with that ID. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
